faq1_question: How long does it take to learn {targetLang}?
faq1_answer: With Lingo7, just 30 minutes a day of parallel reading in {targetLang} leads to noticeable progress within weeks. Consistent daily practice is more effective than long, infrequent study sessions.
faq2_question: Can I learn {targetLang} as a complete beginner?
faq2_answer: Lingo7's parallel reading approach is perfect for {targetLang} beginners. You see the translation right next to the original text, so you can start reading {targetLang} from day one without feeling lost.
faq3_question: What makes Lingo7 different from other {targetLang} learning apps?
faq3_answer: Lingo7 focuses on extensive reading — the most natural and effective way to learn {targetLang}. Instead of isolated exercises, you read real texts with parallel translation, absorbing {targetLang} grammar and vocabulary in context.
faq4_question: Can I learn {targetLang} in my native language?
faq4_answer: Yes! Lingo7 supports 90+ native languages. You can learn {targetLang} with translations in your preferred language.

practice_title: The most proven way to remember words
practice_subtitle: Spaced repetition is one of the most rigorously studied learning techniques of the last century — repeatedly shown to outperform cramming and re-reading by a wide margin. Lingo7 tracks every word you tap while reading and brings it back for review right before you'd forget it. 14 different exercises — from quick recognition to active recall, typing and pronunciation — strengthen memory from every angle.
practice_desc_choose: See a word and pick the correct translation from four options
practice_desc_listen: Listen to a word and choose the correct translation — trains your ear
practice_desc_spell: Build the word letter by letter from scrambled characters
practice_desc_is_correct: See a word and a translation — quickly decide if it's correct
practice_desc_pronounce: Pronounce the word and get instant accuracy feedback
practice_desc_recall: Try to recall the translation from memory before revealing it

faq2_subtitle: The 5 principles every polyglot uses — built into Lingo7.
faq2_item1_principle: Principle: Extensive reading — consuming large volumes of text you can mostly understand — is one of the most research-backed paths to fluency (Krashen's input hypothesis). The trick is removing friction so you can read a lot without stopping.
faq2_item1_how: How Lingo7 helps: Parallel translation sits right next to the original — no dictionary lookups, no breaking flow. Turbo mode highlights words in rhythm if you lose your place, so you can devour pages instead of decoding them.
faq2_item2_question: 02 — Review words at the right moment
faq2_item2_principle: Principle: Spaced repetition is one of the most rigorously studied learning techniques of the last century. The idea: review each word just before you would have forgotten it — that's when a single repetition strengthens memory the most.
faq2_item2_how: How Lingo7 helps: Lingo7's spaced repetition system tracks every word you save and brings it back at the optimal interval. You don't schedule anything — the algorithm handles timing, and 14 different exercises keep memory sharp from every angle.
faq2_item3_question: 03 — Learn only the words you need
faq2_item3_principle: Principle: Polyglots don't memorize every word in order — they focus on the ones that pay off. High-frequency words dominate everyday language: a few hundred of the most common words are enough to start understanding real books, conversations, and articles.
faq2_item3_how: How Lingo7 helps: Two frequency-ranked starter collections built from corpus data — the 100 most popular words to get off the ground, then 250 more for real traction. Available in 70+ languages. Learn the minimum that gives you the maximum, instead of chasing a dictionary you'll never finish.
faq2_item4_principle: Principle: Boring textbooks kill motivation. Polyglots choose texts they actually enjoy — favorite books, articles, scripts — because interest is what sustains daily practice for months and years, not willpower.
faq2_item4_how: How Lingo7 helps: A large, growing library across genres — classic novels, contemporary articles, topic summaries. You pick what you actually care about, not what a textbook assigns. Interest does the hard work of keeping you consistent.
faq2_item5_principle: Principle: The fastest progress happens when reading, listening, vocabulary, and pronunciation reinforce each other. A word you've read, heard, and pronounced is remembered in a way that isolated drilling can't match — overall results grow faster than the sum of individual exercises.
faq2_item5_how: How Lingo7 helps: One session covers all four: you read a page with parallel translation, listen to the narration with word-by-word highlighting, tap new words to save them, and practice pronunciation on the ones you learn. Four skills trained in parallel, in 7–15 minutes a day.
testimonials_subtitle: Real stories from language learners around the world
testimonial_1: That feeling when you're not 'studying' but just reading and suddenly understanding — priceless. Really well done.
testimonial_2: Parallel reading is literally the most convenient way to learn a language without the pain.
testimonial_3: Learning here feels natural: context is key. When you see the full phrase, it sticks easier and without forcing yourself.
testimonial_4: Finally, learning that doesn't frustrate you: just read and absorb.
testimonial_5: For me it's not about 'learning words' but keeping my language sharp. It's convenient to read regularly and stay in context — great tool for maintaining your level.
testimonial_6: A very comfortable way to build vocabulary. I started translating less word-by-word in my head and understanding more by meaning.
testimonial_7: I'm starting from zero, and it's important not to drown in rules. Here I just read in small chunks and don't panic — having the translation nearby really helps.
testimonial_8: Nice that the app isn't overloaded with extras. You just read and gradually start recognizing words and patterns. A calm, productive process.
testimonial_9: I love that you don't have to depend on motivation. Even when tired, just reading a couple of paragraphs is already practice.
testimonial_10: Why didn't I find this earlier? I finally have a normal rhythm: open — 15 minutes — close, and I actually learned.
testimonial_11: Open — read — understand. Everything I needed.
testimonial_12: For intermediate level this is great: you reinforce grammar and vocabulary with real examples. You start catching fixed expressions faster.
testimonial_13: I'm thrilled with the feeling of 'I'm really reading'. Not exercises for exercises' sake, but living language, and my brain starts putting the puzzle together on its own.
testimonial_14: Reading in a foreign language used to be a punishment, now it's actually enjoyable. Parallel text saves you from getting stuck.
testimonial_15: I liked that the focus is on understanding the text. Having the translation nearby helps you not lose the flow and learn in context. It's really more convenient than cramming.
testimonial_16: Text + translation side by side = the perfect format for real practice.
testimonial_17: I like that you can study in small sessions. Even 20-30 minutes feels productive because it's real texts and real language.
testimonial_18: I thought I'd quit again after two days... but this actually hooks you. You read and go: 'Oh, I got it!' — and want just one more bit.
testimonial_19: At an advanced level, volume and quality of contact with the language is what matters. Here it happens naturally: you read a lot without friction.
testimonial_20: Well made: open a text, read, quickly check and continue. That's why you don't get tired and don't quit after three days.
testimonial_21: Very easy to get into — a little bit every day, stress-free.
testimonial_22: Minimum fluff, maximum value — I love apps like this.
testimonial_23: I actually stopped jumping between dictionaries and losing the meaning. Reading became whole, and progress is more noticeable because of it.
testimonial_24: Really love the 'read and learn' format. No prep, no searching for materials — just open and practice. Perfect for busy people.
testimonial_25: My favorite moment is when you suddenly catch yourself reading faster and stumbling less on every word. It's very motivating!
testimonial_26: With this format I stopped getting stuck in the dictionary every minute.
testimonial_27: I find it hard to start because everything seems confusing. But here I see the meaning right away — and gain confidence to continue.
testimonial_28: I read with pleasure — and it suddenly became my 'lesson'.
testimonial_29: This is the first app where I don't feel stupid. No panic from unfamiliar words — the translation is right there, and you keep going.
testimonial_30: I already know the basics, but I was missing regular practice. This format fits perfectly into my day: I read and level up my comprehension without extra prep.
